title: malva parviflora var. oxyloba
intro: Malva parviflora var. oxyloba, also known as mallow, is a plant species belonging to the family Malvaceae, characterized by its small, white flowers and heart-shaped leaves. The plant is rich in mucilages, which are complex carbohydrates that have medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and soothing effects. The health benefits of Malva parviflora var. oxyloba include its ability to calm digestive issues, reduce inflammation, and alleviate respiratory problems such as coughs and sore throats. Malva parviflora var. oxyloba can be prepared as a tea, infusion, or tincture, and is often used in herbal medicine to support the body's natural healing processes.
